scenario.context: Station 01: Hardware profile and follow-up tickets in several waves.
scenario.mission: You handle the initial assignment and multiple follow-up tasks for the same workstation group.

station.profile: Office, browser, email, video conferencing, 2 monitors

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: initial workstation profile assignment
customerRequest.body:
Please start by selecting a viable hardware profile for sales station NS-V2-WS-03.

After that, additional customer tasks for the same station follow with increasing responsibility (compatibility, cost, prioritization, handover).

questions.title: Baseline selection for the profile
questions.prompt: Which configuration best fits office + browser + video conferencing with 2 monitors?

options.text: 4 GB RAM, HDD, one video output
options.rationale: Too weak and too inflexible for the profile.

options.text: 8 GB RAM, SSD, one video output
options.rationale: Memory is ok for basics, but ports are not suitable for 2 monitors.

options.text: 16 GB RAM, SSD, dock with 2 video outputs
options.rationale: Fits the workload profile and peripheral needs.

feedback.correct: Correct: the combination covers performance and ports.
feedback.wrong: Not viable: either performance or connectivity is insufficient.

questions.title: Package cost for 5 stations
questions.prompt: Per station: PC 580 EUR, 2 monitors at 155 EUR each, dock 88 EUR, setup 0.6 h at 60 EUR/h. What is the total for 5 stations (excluding tax)?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please sum materials and time per station cleanly first.

questions.title: Mandatory criteria for approval
questions.prompt: Which points must be met for technical approval? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Current updates and baseline protection enabled
Login test with company account successful
Only case appearance assessed
options.rationale: Appearance is not an approval criterion.

options.text: Short functional test documented
Private extensions installed without approval

feedback.correct: Correct: you prioritize security and operational criteria.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: please weigh hard approval criteria more strongly.

questions.title: Short briefing to the team lead
questions.prompt: Write a short briefing (4-6 sentences) with the chosen solution, the main justification, and one residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear recommendation, professional justification, one realistic residual risk.

feedback.correct: Correct: the briefing is clear and decision-ready.
feedback.wrong: Please separate solution, justification and residual risk more clearly.

questions.title: Start sequence in station 01
questions.prompt: Arrange the start sequence sensibly.

feedback.correct: Correct: the sequence is technically clean.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: please establish need and checks first, then approval.

questions.title: Follow-up task 1 - Priority shift due to new software
questions.subject: New CRM version increases resource demand
questions.body: A new CRM version is being rolled out immediately. Please check whether the current hardware decision still provides enough performance headroom and adjust the recommendation if needed.

questions.title: Impact on the hardware choice
questions.prompt: Which adjustments make professional sense for a higher workload profile? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Plan RAM headroom
Make SSD instead of HDD mandatory
Require a third monitor for everyone
options.rationale: Only sensible if the need exists.

options.text: Check CPU class for a viable mid-range
options.rationale: Processor performance must match the workload.

options.text: Ignore power supply capacity entirely
options.rationale: Core components must not be neglected.

feedback.correct: Correct: you respond purposefully to the changed load.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: tie workload profile and technical viability more tightly.

questions.title: Quick plausibility check
questions.prompt: Which statement best fits as the first plausibility check?

options.text: Compare application profile, concurrent use and peripherals against the technical data
options.rationale: Fast, professionally clean starting point for re-evaluation.

options.text: Only compare the current price with the old quote
options.rationale: Price without technical context is not sufficient.

options.text: Automatically choose high-end components

feedback.correct: Correct: first align need and technology cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not fitting: the direct tech-to-need comparison is missing.

questions.title: Re-evaluation in 5 sentences
questions.prompt: Formulate a short re-evaluation with changed load, adjustment and justification.
questions.expectedOutline: Old vs. new, concrete adjustment, clear justification based on usage.

feedback.correct: Correct: the re-evaluation is plausible and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please connect workload, adjustment and justification more clearly.

questions.title: Additional cost for a second monitor
questions.prompt: Out of 18 employees, 11 need a second monitor. One additional monitor costs 149 EUR. What are the additional costs?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply the required quantity by the unit price.

questions.title: Implement change cleanly
questions.prompt: Put the steps in a sensible order when requirements increase during the ongoing assignment.

feedback.correct: Correct: the change is secured technically and commercially.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: structure analysis, cost and alignment more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 2 - SSD supply shortage
questions.subject: Alternative configurations with the same target effect
questions.body: There is a short-term supply shortage for a specific SSD model. Please evaluate viable alternatives without compromising the station's ability to work.

questions.title: Best response to the supply shortage
questions.prompt: Which approach makes the most sense for an SSD supply shortage?

options.text: Switch back to HDD so the price stays the same
options.rationale: The technical target effect would drop significantly.

options.text: Check an alternative SSD with suitable performance and warranty parameters
options.rationale: Preserves the target effect with controlled adjustment.

options.text: Stop the entire assignment and do not continue

feedback.correct: Correct: the functional goal remains intact and the risk stays manageable.
feedback.wrong: Not goal-oriented: the supply issue must be solved with a technical alternative.

questions.title: Evaluate a replacement product
questions.prompt: Arrange the evaluation flow for a replacement product.
items: Approve or reject the replacement product
List must-have criteria from the main assignment
Match technical data of the replacement product
correctOrder: List must-have criteria from the main assignment
Match technical data of the replacement product
Approve or reject the replacement product

feedback.correct: Correct: criteria-based replacement decision.
feedback.wrong: Please secure criteria first, then run the replacement check.

questions.title: Risk reduction for replacement parts
questions.prompt: Which measures reduce risk with replacement parts? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Check compatibility against existing peripherals
Compare warranty and support conditions
options.rationale: Important for operations and follow-up costs.

options.text: Look only at delivery time
options.rationale: Delivery time alone is not enough.

options.text: Run a short test with a reference configuration
Approve the product without a datasheet

feedback.correct: Correct: you combine data quality, testing and support view.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: consider technical and operational risks together.

questions.title: Justify the replacement decision
questions.prompt: Write a short decision note (4-6 sentences) explaining why the alternative is approved or rejected.
questions.expectedOutline: Criteria reference, clear judgment, short risk note.

feedback.correct: Correct: the note is suitable as an approval basis.
feedback.wrong: Please connect alternative, criteria and decision more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 3 - Budget cap from procurement
questions.subject: Tighten cost cap per workstation
questions.body: Controlling sets a hard cost cap per workstation. Please optimize the configuration so that quality and minimum criteria remain intact.

questions.title: Calculate reserve uplift
questions.prompt: A configuration costs 4,250 EUR. A 12% reserve is added for uncertainty. What is the total value including the reserve?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please apply the percentage uplift cleanly to the base value.

questions.title: Reasonable saving measure
questions.prompt: Which saving measure is most defensible without missing the workload profile?

options.text: Reduce RAM from 16 GB to 4 GB
Replace SSD with an old HDD
options.rationale: Significantly worsens boot and response times.

options.text: Choose a suitable but cheaper dock model with the same core functions

feedback.correct: Correct: costs drop while the core function remains.
feedback.wrong: Not goal-oriented: saving must not break minimum criteria.

questions.title: Minimum criteria despite budget pressure
questions.prompt: Which points must not be removed despite the budget cap? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Stable SSD-based system drive
Matching monitor ports for 2 displays
Any gaming add-on features
options.rationale: Not required for the use case.

options.text: Security and update capability
A third monitor without need

feedback.correct: Correct: core requirements remain intact.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: budget optimization must not override minimum criteria.

questions.title: Structured budget adjustment
questions.prompt: Arrange the flow for a late budget cap.
items: Look for savings without functional loss
Align the change with the client
correctOrder: Look for savings without functional loss
Align the change with the client

feedback.correct: Correct: the approach is professionally robust.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first minimum criteria, then savings and alignment.

questions.title: Budget update to the customer
questions.prompt: Formulate a short budget update (4-6 sentences) with adjustment, impact and remaining risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Which adjustment, which technical impact, which residual risk remains.

feedback.correct: Correct: the update is decision-ready.
feedback.wrong: Please present adjustment, impact and risk more clearly.

questions.title: Edge-case decision
questions.prompt: A quote is 4% over budget but reduces later support time significantly. What is the most professional approach?

options.text: Reject immediately just because the budget is exceeded
options.rationale: Can be uneconomical in the long run.

options.text: Present the deviation transparently with a justified benefit comparison
options.rationale: Professional: make short- and long-term effects explicit.

options.text: Approve automatically without documentation
options.rationale: A decision without evidence is not robust.

feedback.correct: Correct: benefits and deviation must be weighed transparently.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: edge cases require transparent justification.

questions.title: Follow-up task 4 - Immediate handover today 15:00
questions.subject: Acceptance today, remaining items tomorrow
questions.body: The station must be handed over to the business unit today by 15:00. Please prioritize acceptance criteria, document remaining items cleanly and plan the follow-up without operational risk.

questions.title: Prioritized handover under time pressure
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean handover by 15:00.
items: Short alignment with the business unit
correctOrder: Short alignment with the business unit

feedback.correct: Correct: prioritization and evidence are combined cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: under time pressure, must-have tests and documentation come first.

questions.title: Leasing total cost
questions.prompt: Leasing rate 79 EUR per month and workstation, 7 workstations, term 24 months. What is the total cost?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply monthly rate, number of stations and term correctly.

questions.title: Handle remaining items correctly
questions.prompt: Which statements describe a clean handling of remaining items? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Document remaining items with owner and due date
Leave remaining items open without an entry
Briefly state the risk impact
For security-critical items, no approval without a countermeasure
Mark all remaining items as done by default

feedback.correct: Correct: remaining items are handled in a controllable and auditable way.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: remaining items need due date, responsibility and risk classification.

questions.title: Final status message for station 01
questions.prompt: Write a final status message (5-7 sentences) with status, open items, risk and next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Closure with a clear situation, documented open items and a concrete follow-up plan.

feedback.correct: Correct: the final message is directly usable for operations.
feedback.wrong: Please phrase status, open items and next step more clearly and concretely.
